title: viola hirta var. typica
intro: Viola hirta var. typica, also known as the hairy pansy, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the viola genus and is native to Europe, particularly in the mountainous regions. The hairy pansy has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. The hairy pansy can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall health and well-being, and to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
